channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby says her 'little princess' was 'truly loved'
item.description: At a vigil held in Alice Springs last night for Kumanjayi Little Baby, a statement was read on behalf of her mother who called for the community to remember her five-year-old child as a "pretty girl in pink" who was "truly loved" by her family.
item.dc:creator: Grace Atta and Alex Barwick

media:group.media:description: Alice Springs residents gathered at ANZAC Oval for a vigil in honour of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: Beyonce's hip-hop film debut walked so that Hamilton could fly
item.description: Carmen: A Hip Hopera starred superstar Beyonce in her acting debut. It's been 25 years since the now cult classic musical drama aired on MTV in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Mawunyo Gbogbo

media:group.media:description: Beyoncé, already famous at the time, has gone on to become one of the biggest superstars in the world.

item.title: Cost of living relief in WA budget labelled 'a joke' by some regional residents
item.description: Residents in regional Western Australia say the 2026–27 State Budget fails to provide any significant cost of living relief to those living outside of Perth or major regional cities.
item.dc:creator: Madigan Landry, Andrew Williams, and Dunja Karagic

media:group.media:description: WA Treasurer Rita Saffioti and Premier Roger Cook say the 2026–27 State Budget focused on cost of living.

item.title: What is known about the IS-linked families that arrived in Australia
item.description: Women and children linked to the Islamic State terrorist group have arrived in Melbourne and Sydney. Here's who they are and what awaits them.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Courty and Rudi Maxwell

media:group.media:description: Women who left the Al Roj camp in Syria walking through Melbourne Airport after landing in Australia.

item.title: How the Aussies shape up ahead of the Giro d'Italia
item.description: The 109th Giro d'Italia gets underway in Bulgaria on Friday, with 12 Aussies competing for glory in the first grand tour of the 2026 season. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Jonas Vingegaard is heading into the Giro as heavy favourite.

item.title: Cockatoo removed from city's aviary amid welfare concerns
item.description: Orange City Council says it is meeting all the requirements of its licence to operate the Cook Park aviary but concerned locals say it is time the enclosure was closed for good.
item.dc:creator: Emily Middleton

media:group.media:description: Jeffery, the cockatoo, was taken to a nearby vet.

item.title: Investigation finds mining company did not clear land illegally
item.description: The federal government says Magnetic South's land clearing last year was not illegal, but a conservation group says FOI documents raise questions over the validity of the decision.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Beavan

media:group.media:description: It's been estimated that around 200 hectares of land was cleared last year.

item.title: Week-long police blitz targets organised crime on NSW–Victoria border
item.description: Police from Victoria and NSW spend a week on the Victoria–NSW border targeting outlaw motorcycle gangs and organised crime.

media:group.media:description: A 26-year-old Yarrawonga man was arrested during the week-long operation.

item.title: Gastric bypass patient left with malnutrition still waiting for investigation
item.description: Chinei Ward feels "very defeated" after battling complications from her gastric bypass surgery and a lengthy complaints process that still has no end in sight.
item.dc:creator: Johanna Marie

media:group.media:description: Chinei Ward has lodged a complaint with Ahpra about her surgeon.

item.title: Saffioti walks WA budget tightrope as global conditions worsen
item.description: Even with another eye-watering surplus, the uber-wealthy Western Australian government can't deliver its population one of the biggest things people want: certainty in an uncertain world.
item.dc:creator: Keane Bourke

media:group.media:description: Rita Saffioti is warning global economic headwinds are gaining strength.

item.title: US trade court rules against Trump's 10pc tariff rate
item.description: A United States trade court rules against President Donald Trump's universal 10 per cent tariff rate, dealing another blow to his signature economic policy.

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump ordered 10 per cent tariffs on most imports to the US earlier this year.

item.title: Mother's Day can reopen painful wounds. Here's how to start healing
item.description: It's the yearning ache that comes from reckoning with the imperfect human mother we get, against the idealised fantasy version we wish for.
item.dc:creator: Beverley Wang

media:group.media:description: Evelyn and Joy have a rather strained relationship in the film Everything Everywhere All At Once.

item.title: Smith given 'every assurance' of LIV survival
item.description: Cameron Smith says the LIV golf league will continue past 2026 despite funding issues.

media:group.media:description: Cameron Smith says LIV will survive.

item.title: $1b worth of forgotten roads have been 'found' by Queensland councils
item.description: A Queensland Audit Office report finds a $1.34 billion discrepancy between what appeared on council books and maps.
item.dc:creator: Kenji Sato

media:group.media:description: A billion dollars worth of roads and paths have gone unaccounted-for since 2020.

item.title: LeBron's new gear at 41 leads lessons from first round of NBA Playoffs
item.description: LeBron James shows you can never count out a team he is on, even at 41, as the first round of the playoffs wraps up.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva

media:group.media:description: LeBron James's ability to still wind back the clock was one of the key lessons from the first round of the NBA Playoffs.

item.title: Call for major banks to invest in First Nations community-led hub
item.description: A First Nations financial counselling service wants Australia's big four banks to collectively invest in an Indigenous-owned and led banking hub in remote Western Australia.
item.dc:creator: Esse Deves

media:group.media:description: Bernadette Angus said empowering her community through a banking hub was important for the future.

item.title: Live: US and Iran reportedly exchange fire in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran's semi-official news agency is reporting Tehran's navy has targeted three US destroyers in the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Georgie Hewson

media:group.media:description: Cargo ships, including bulk carriers and general cargo vessels, sit at anchor offshore in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Where have Tasmania's rentals gone? New graph highlights housing problem
item.description: A recent housing report highlighted an alarming statistic: Tasmania has less than half the number of vacant rentals than it did a decade ago. So what's behind the decrease?
item.dc:creator: Clancy Balen

media:group.media:description: Tasmania now has less than half the number of vacant rentals than it did a decade ago, according to analysis by Anglicare.

item.title: Dressing room fight leaves Real Madrid captain in hospital
item.description: Teammates Federico Valverde and Aurélien Tchouaméni are involved in an altercation at Real Madrid training that left skipper Valverde in hospital with a head trauma.

media:group.media:description: Federico Valverde (right) was hospitalised after a dressing room bust up with Aurélien Tchouaméni (left).

item.title: Bin night across Australia is changing as councils, households fight waste
item.description: How councils collect rubbish is changing as the cost of disposing of waste skyrockets and they scramble to meet environmental targets.
item.dc:creator: Sam Bradbrook

media:group.media:description: Waste management services are looking to reduce landfill for environmental and economic reasons.

item.title: Live: ASX drops following losses on Wall Street
item.description: The ASX200 has fallen at the open, while reports of the US and Iran exchanging fire in Strait of Hormuz have raised oil prices. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller and Lin Lin

media:group.media:description: March has been a month to forget for investors on Australia's ASX 200.

item.title: Live: Nationals leader concedes it will be an 'uphill battle' to win Farrer by-election
item.description: Voters in the federal seat of Farrer will head to the polls tomorrow to chose a replacement for former opposition leader Sussan Ley but Matt Canavan says it will be an uphill battle for the Nationals to win back. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Gould

item.title: Marsh hits rapid IPL century as Lucknow snap giant losing streak
item.description: Mitchell Marsh smashed a brutal century to help Lucknow Super Giants win just their third match of the IPL season as Virat Kohli fails in Royal Challengers Bengaluru's chase.

media:group.media:description: Mitchell Marsh walloped 111 off 56 balls.

item.title: Dance performs true story of Stolen Generations identity struggle
item.description: A dance that tells the story of a displaced Gurindji man's struggle with identity has travelled the country and is now showing at the Sydney Opera House.
item.dc:creator: Dianne King
item.category: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

media:group.media:description: The performance, choreographed by Gary Lang, is based on a number of letters penned to a researcher.

item.title: International Olympic Committee lifts restrictions on Belarusian athletes
item.description: The International Olympic Committee has lifted its restrictions on Belarusian athletes competing under their own flag, although World Athletics has said it will keep its ban in place.

media:group.media:description: Belarus last marched under their own flag at the 2022 Winter Olympic Games.
